resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Low risk; less likely to have COPD; 18th percentile; based on 892,416 genetic variants
resultRows.description: The public sample page shows a Lung Health card with a low-risk label, COPD wording, percentile graphic, and a visible top-variants table.
genes: Risk model variants shown in sample table
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Lung Health. Use this as Respiratory Health output-format evidence only; it is not a local diagnosis, respiratory risk estimate, lung-function assessment, air-pollution recommendation, supplement recommendation, medication recommendation, testing recommendation, emergency guidance, or treatment-change instruction.

practicalNextSteps: Review respiratory questions with a clinician who can use symptoms, spirometry, oxygen saturation, imaging, medications, and full clinical context.
Use the appendix to see which respiratory model, clinical, and organ-system inputs were unavailable.
Do not use sample risk cards or recommendation rows alone to change supplements, medications, testing, exercise, environmental exposure, or treatment.

probabilities.label: Calibrated personal probability
probabilities.note: The deterministic report body does not quantify disease risk, diagnosis probability, treatment response, recommendation benefit, or actionability probabilities.

uncertainty: Sample-report rows are output-format evidence, not local clinical or lifestyle decisions.
Derived or unavailable-model evidence does not include the clinical, lab, environment, medication, and professional-review context needed for personal decisions.
Missing inputs are listed explicitly instead of being inferred.
missingInputs: Authenticated marketplace detail-page extraction is not available yet.

Respiratory diagnosis, symptoms, spirometry, oxygen saturation, imaging, infection history, medication list, exercise tolerance, pediatric context, clinician review, and calibrated respiratory model are not supplied.
Personal air-pollution, vitamin D, supplement, medication, testing, emergency, and treatment recommendation context is unavailable.
limitations: This local artifact uses public sample-report structure and synthetic unavailable-model evidence for validation.
It does not diagnose asthma, COPD, cystic fibrosis, infection, allergy, sleep apnea, pulmonary disease, or respiratory conditions.

It does not include spirometry, oxygen saturation, imaging, symptoms, infection history, medication list, exercise tolerance, pediatric context, calibrated respiratory model, or clinician interpretation.

organSystemConditionContext.plainEnglishMeaning: Respiratory health context depends on genetics plus symptoms, spirometry, oxygen saturation, imaging, infection history, medication list, age, environment, and clinician review.
unsupportedClaims: asthma, COPD, infection, or lung-function prediction
supplement, medication, testing, emergency, exposure, exercise, or treatment recommendation
organSystemConditionContext.professionalReview: A clinician can interpret respiratory genetics together with symptoms, spirometry, imaging, oxygen saturation, medications, environmental exposures, and full clinical context.
